title: Fix maintenance mode toggle and registered users loading in admin panel

requirements.summary: Fix the maintenance mode toggle in UsersControlsPanel so that calling toggleMaintenanceMode via the admin actor succeeds without throwing an error
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the maintenance mode toggle in the admin panel does not show 'Failed to update maintenance mode' error
The toggle correctly switches between 'App is Live' and 'Maintenance Mode' states
The backend toggleMaintenanceMode function accepts the admin actor call without rejection
The updated maintenance mode state persists and is reflected on next load

file_operations.description: Replace the actor-based toggleMaintenanceMode call with an adminActor call. Import useAdminActor hook and use it to obtain the admin actor instance. Ensure adminActor is initialized before calling toggleMaintenanceMode. Update the mutation to use adminActor.toggleMaintenanceMode() instead of actor.toggleMaintenanceMode(). Add proper error handling for cases where adminActor is not yet initialized. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

Review and ensure the admin actor singleton is correctly initialized with admin credentials (PIN token from sessionStorage). Verify that the actor initialization properly sets up the identity with admin privileges before exposing the actor instance. Ensure that the loading state accurately reflects whether the admin actor is ready for use. Add defensive checks to prevent returning an uninitialized actor. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

Add a new mutation hook useToggleMaintenanceMode that accepts an adminActor parameter and calls adminActor.toggleMaintenanceMode(). Include proper error handling with user-friendly error messages. Invalidate the 'maintenanceMode' query key on success to refresh the UI state. Return the mutation object with isLoading, error, and mutate properties for UI feedback. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

requirements.summary: Fix the registered users list in UsersControlsPanel so that getAllUsers is called via a correctly initialized admin actor with proper permissions
acceptanceCriteria: The Registered Users section displays the list of registered users without showing 'Failed to load users' error
Users can be searched by Unique ID or name
The backend user listing function authorizes the admin actor call and returns all users
Block/unblock actions remain functional after the fix

file_operations.description: Replace the actor-based getAllUsers query with an adminActor-based query. Use the adminActor instance from useAdminActor hook. Ensure the users query is only enabled when adminActor is initialized and not in a loading state. Update the useGetAllUsers call to pass adminActor as a parameter if needed, or create a new admin-specific query hook. Ensure block/unblock mutations also use adminActor for their operations. Add proper loading and error states that distinguish between adminActor initialization and data fetching. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.

Create or modify the useGetAllUsers hook to accept an optional adminActor parameter. When adminActor is provided, use it instead of the regular actor for the getAllUsers query. Ensure the query is only enabled when the actor (or adminActor) is available and properly initialized. Add error handling that distinguishes between authorization errors and other failures. Return clear error messages that help diagnose whether the issue is with actor initialization or backend permissions. Verify the authorization component's usage instructions before implementing.
